full_name: SAND2017-6925 - Recommended Practices for Abuse Testing Rechargeable Energy Storage Systems (RESSs)
scope: Rechargeable energy storage systems (RESSs) for EV, PHEV, and HEV applications.
important_note: This document explicitly does NOT define pass/fail criteria. It recommends abuse test procedures, measurements, and hazard severity reporting for design evaluation.

general_test_conditions.assembly_level: Test at the lowest meaningful level of assembly first.
general_test_conditions.system_age: Prefer BOL test articles; slightly used articles are acceptable in early design stages.
general_test_conditions.data_collection: ~1 Hz generally; kHz recommended for short circuit initial seconds and impact/crush force signals.

evaluation_basis.hazard_severity_levels: Use modified HSL 0-7 scale instead of mandatory pass/fail.
evaluation_basis.flammability_analysis: Use ignition sources and vent-gas analysis to determine flammability where relevant.
evaluation_basis.hazardous_substance_monitoring: Reference AIHA ERPG-2 or comparable short-term exposure guidance where practical.

recommendations.purpose: Determine abuse response to mechanical crush under controlled low-speed conditions and report hazard severity at defined force/displacement levels.

suggested_conditions.cell_cylindrical: Crush at 1 mm/min with scaled cylindrical impactor. Stop at 25 kN, HSL ≥5, or 100% practical displacement.
suggested_conditions.cell_prismatic_pouch: Crush in constrained fixture at 1 mm/min using semicylindrical steel impactor. Stop at 25 kN, HSL ≥5, or 100% practical displacement.
suggested_conditions.module_pack: Crush at 1 mm/min between flat and textured platen with 75 mm radius semicylinder. Report displacement and HSL at 100 kN.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: No fixed pass/fail. Report HSL at key force/displacement points, especially 100 kN for module/pack tests.
recommendations.rationale: Useful for identifying onset and severity of crush-induced failure rather than simple compliance.

recommendations.purpose: Characterize response to conductive rod penetration and internal shorting under varied SOC and temperature conditions.

suggested_conditions.rod: 3 ± 0.2 mm conductive rod, rounded tip radius 1.5 ± 0.1 mm.
suggested_conditions.starting_point: Start at 100% SOC and 55°C, then proceed through lower temperature/SOC matrix if more articles are available.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: No fixed pass/fail. Report HSL before penetration, at penetration, and after penetration.
recommendations.rationale: Designed to map severity across a test matrix rather than certify compliance.

recommendations.purpose: Heat a DUT to failure and quantify consequence magnitude as a function of DUT temperature.

end_conditions: DUT reaches 250°C and holds 15 min without self-heating

recommendations.evaluation_basis: No fixed pass/fail. Report HSL as a function of DUT temperature.
recommendations.rationale: Useful for identifying thermal failure onset and consequence severity.

recommendations.purpose: Measure total heat generation and heating rate under quasi-adiabatic conditions.

suggested_conditions.setup: Suspend DUT in ARC without direct heater contact; instrument with thermocouple on cell skin/tab.
suggested_conditions.example_threshold: Exotherm threshold 0.02°C/min; max temperature 405°C for a 2.0 Ah cylindrical Li-ion cell.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report maximum temperature, heating rate versus time/temperature, and vent gas pressure when applicable.
recommendations.rationale: Useful for thermal kinetics, not direct pass/fail compliance.

recommendations.purpose: Evaluate response to constant-current overcharge using realistic but not excessively aggressive currents.

suggested_conditions.charge_current: Tiered by capacity per Table 8: generally 1C / 2C, or 32 A / 80 A where appropriate.
suggested_conditions.limits: Cell limit 20 V; module limit 20 V per cell/parallel group; pack limit 1.5× pack voltage.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report HSL at key SOC values, including 200% SOC and failure point.
recommendations.rationale: Document emphasizes realistic design-learning over worst-case destruction.

recommendations.item_name_zh: 過電壓建議（電化學電容）
recommendations.purpose: Evaluate electrochemical capacitor response to high-rate charge and voltage excursion.

end_conditions: 2× rated voltage + 15 min hold without self-heating

recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report HSL at end of test.
recommendations.rationale: Only intended for electrochemical capacitors, not lithium batteries.

recommendations.purpose: Evaluate response to deep discharge and possible voltage reversal.

suggested_conditions.cell: 1C to -20 V compliance or until 1.5 h / HSL ≥5.
suggested_conditions.module_pack: 1C until 1.5 h, all subassemblies reversed for 15 min, or HSL ≥5.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report HSL at end of test.
recommendations.rationale: Focus is design vulnerability, not compliance acceptance.

recommendations.item_name_zh: 電壓反轉建議（電化學電容）
recommendations.purpose: Evaluate capacitor response to reversal beyond zero volts.

suggested_conditions.sequence: Charge at 5C to rated voltage, then discharge at 5C to negative rated voltage.
end_conditions: hold negative rated voltage for 15 min

recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report HSL at end of test.

recommendations.purpose: Determine DUT response to hard, medium, and optionally soft external short loads relative to DUT DC resistance.

suggested_conditions.practical_floor: For DUT resistance <5 mΩ, use 1-5 mΩ practical load and document it.
suggested_conditions.application_time: Apply load in <1 s for minimum 60 min; longer duration may be needed for module/pack.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report HSL at end of test.
recommendations.rationale: Relative short severity is more informative than a single fixed resistor.

recommendations.purpose: Trigger a single-cell thermal runaway under otherwise normal conditions and evaluate cell-to-cell propagation.

suggested_conditions.precondition: First determine a reproducible single-cell trigger at cell level.
suggested_conditions.control_modes: Test with active controls enabled and disabled; keep passive controls in place.
suggested_conditions.monitoring_period: If no propagation (HSL ≤4), monitor ≥60 min. If propagation occurs, monitor an additional 60 min from last failure event.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report extent of propagation, failure modes of affected cells, HSL at subunit and highest assembly levels, and propagation duration.
recommendations.rationale: Core design-level recommendation for understanding propagation vulnerability.

recommendations.purpose: Determine whether vent gas ignites and how long it burns.

suggested_conditions.base_method: Variant of Thermal Ramp with ignition source(s) placed near known or expected vent path.
end_conditions: If sustained ignition occurs, stop heating and wait for self-extinguishment
Otherwise continue to 250°C and hold 15 min

recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report presence/absence of sustained ignition, ignition delay, ignition temperature, and total burn time.
recommendations.rationale: Supports comparison of vent gas ignition risk separate from mandatory compliance tests.

recommendations.purpose: Evaluate voltage stability after separator shutdown and elevated thermal exposure.

suggested_conditions.heating_rate: 2-5°C/min until shutdown temperature + 5°C, then step higher in 5°C increments if needed.
suggested_conditions.post_shutdown_stress: Apply 20 Vdc at max 1 A for 30 min after shutdown is confirmed.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report HSL, peak leakage current, and duration of applied voltage.
recommendations.rationale: Only applicable where shutdown separator technology exists.

recommendations.purpose: Evaluate RESS behavior during seawater-like immersion and monitor hydrogen generation or thermal response.

methods: fill vessel with salt water in <5 min after DUT placement

suggested_measurements: temperature (1 kHz first 5 s, then 1 Hz)
recommendations.evaluation_basis: Report HSL at end of test.
recommendations.rationale: Important for field failure scenarios such as flooding or marine exposure.

recommendations.purpose: Understand RESS response to direct fire exposure.

suggested_conditions.note: Direct fuel fire is destructive and may limit monitoring instrumentation survival.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: No fixed pass/fail defined in the report; treat as characterization guidance.

recommendations.purpose: Use radiant heat as a more instrument-friendly surrogate for direct fuel fire exposure.

suggested_conditions.heat_source: Radiant heat source simulating fuel fire conditions.

recommendations.evaluation_basis: No fixed pass/fail defined; intended for comparative understanding of response under fire-like thermal load.
recommendations.rationale: Useful when direct fuel fire would destroy instrumentation needed for data capture.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: 1 mm/min controlled crush; scaled impactor; report force/displacement, especially 100 kN for module/pack.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by HSL at defined force/displacement milestones.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: 3 mm conductive rod, 0.1 mm/s, start at 100% SOC and 55°C, then expand through SOC/temperature matrix.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by HSL before/at/after penetration and by voltage/temperature response.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Heat at 2-5°C/min from normal temperature at 100% SOC until 250°C hold or failure.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by HSL versus DUT temperature.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: 5°C heat steps with 30-minute waits; characterize heating rate and exotherm onset in ARC.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by max temperature, self-heating rate, and pressure if available.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Realistic tiered current matrix by DUT capacity; stop at 250% SOC or failure.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by HSL at 200% SOC and failure point.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Electrochemical capacitors only; ≥10C to 2× rated voltage with 15-minute hold.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by end-of-test HSL.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: 1C discharge; cell to -20 V, module/pack until reversal criteria or 1.5 h.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by end-of-test HSL.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Electrochemical capacitors only; 5C charge to rated voltage then 5C discharge to negative rated voltage.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by end-of-test HSL.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Hard/medium/soft short based on DUT DC resistance; minimum 60-minute application.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by HSL and current/temperature response under relative short severity.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Trigger single-cell thermal runaway at 100% SOC/25°C; test with active controls enabled and disabled.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by extent of propagation, duration, and HSL at subunit/highest assembly levels.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Thermal ramp with ignition source(s) near vent path; monitor ignition delay and burn duration.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by sustained ignition yes/no, ignition temperature, delay, and burn time.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Heat to separator shutdown, then apply 20 Vdc / 1 A for 30 min.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by leakage current, shutdown behavior, and HSL.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: 3.5% NaCl immersion at 100% SOC for minimum 2 h or until severe failure.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: No pass/fail. Compare by HSL and gas/isolation response, including hydrogen generation.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Direct fuel-fire exposure, referencing ECE R100 Annex 8E.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: Characterization only; no fixed pass/fail in this report.

comparison_items.conditions_summary: Radiant heat source used as a fire surrogate to preserve instrumentation capability.
comparison_items.evaluation_summary: Characterization only; no fixed pass/fail in this report.

metadata.extraction_method: Manual reading and restructuring of complete .md file into guidance + comparison-adapter schema
metadata.web_intent: Selectable in future standard comparison UI through comparison_items[] while preserving native guidance semantics in recommendations[].
metadata.notes: This file intentionally avoids fake pass/fail criteria. It preserves SAND2017 as a recommended-practice document while still supporting cross-document comparison in the future web UI.
